title: Run HB-Stan CV sampling and finalize all folds in one call
summary: Run HB-MNL sampling via CmdStan across all folds in a cross-validation directory, immediately finalizing each fold to produce per-fold diagnostics and part-worth exports, then write the aggregate CV summary — all in one call. Equivalent to running cv-fit then cv-finalize back-to-back.
description: Convenience wrapper that runs `cv-fit` followed immediately by `cv-finalize` in a single protocol envelope. For each fold subdirectory in folds_dir it runs the full CSV → Stan JSON → NUTS sampling pipeline, then finalizes the fold's chain CSVs to produce diagnostics.json, summary.json, and part_worths.csv. After all folds complete, writes the aggregate cv_summary at the top of run_dir. Use this when you want the complete CV deliverable in one step; use `cv-fit` + `cv-finalize` separately when you need to inspect per-fold chains before finalizing.
usage:
[["hb-stan::cv-fit-finalize"]]
folds_dir = "<cv-folds dir>"
run_dir = "<dir>"
run_name = "stan_run"
# optional:
headers = true
num_samples = 1000
num_warmup = 500
num_chains = 4
seed = <int>
thin = <int>
parallel_chains = true
prior_lkj_eta = 1.0
sigma_scale = 1.0
ridge_scale = 1e-6

arguments.name: folds_dir
arguments.description: Directory containing fold subdirectories. Each subdirectory must contain a train.csv in coded-long format.

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Output directory. One subdirectory per fold is created here, each containing stan_data.json, chain CSVs, diagnostics.json, summary.json, and part_worths.csv. cv_summary.json and cv_summary.csv are written at the top level.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

arguments.name: headers
arguments.description: Whether train.csv files have a header row. Default true.

arguments.name: num_samples
arguments.description: Post-warmup draws per chain per fold. Default 1000.

arguments.name: num_warmup
arguments.description: Warmup iterations per chain per fold. Default 500.

arguments.name: num_chains
arguments.description: Number of chains per fold. Default 4.

arguments.name: seed
arguments.description: Base RNG seed. Each fold receives seed + fold_index × 1000. Omit for non-deterministic.

arguments.name: thin
arguments.description: Thinning factor. Omit to keep all draws.

arguments.name: parallel_chains
arguments.description: Run chains in parallel within each fold. Default true.

arguments.name: prior_lkj_eta
arguments.description: LKJ prior η, all folds. Default 1.0.

arguments.name: sigma_scale
arguments.description: Half-normal σ prior scale, all folds. Default 1.0.

arguments.name: ridge_scale
arguments.description: Covariance prior ridge scale, all folds. Default 1e-6.

arguments.name: covariate_input
arguments.description: Path to a CSV file with respondent-level covariates. First column must be resp_id (case-insensitive) matching UIDs in the training CSV. Remaining columns are numeric covariates. An intercept column of 1s is prepended automatically. Same file used for all folds. Omit for intercept-only model.

details:
`cv-fit-finalize` is the one-shot cross-validation workflow command. It runs cv-fit followed immediately by cv-finalize in a single protocol envelope. All intermediate files (per-fold stan_data.json, chain CSVs, diagnostics.json, summary.json, part_worths.csv, and the top-level cv_summary) are preserved in run_dir.

Use the two-step form when you need to inspect per-fold chain diagnostics before finalizing, or when you want to re-run cv-finalize without re-doing the sampling.
value:
On success, run_dir contains the full set of cv-fit and cv-finalize outputs:

- One subdirectory per fold with stan_data.json, chain CSVs, diagnostics.json, summary.json, part_worths.csv.
- cv_summary.json and cv_summary.csv at the top level.

In protocol mode the plugin emits event.run.started, per-fold progress events, then event.run.finished (or event.run.failed).
notes: To re-finalize after the fact, run cv-finalize directly — chain CSVs are preserved.
Folds are processed sequentially; total time scales with K × per-fold sampling cost.
CmdStan must be installed and on PATH.

see_also.description: Sampling-only step — use when you need to inspect chains before finalizing.
Produces the folds_dir layout this command consumes.

examples.title: CV with more sampling and reproducible seed
examples.body:
[["hb-stan::cv-fit-finalize"]]
folds_dir = "./cv-folds"
run_dir = "./runs/hb-stan-cv-long"
run_name = "stan_run2"
num_samples = 2000
num_warmup = 1000
seed = 42

examples.title: CV with conservative prior for a sparse study
examples.body:
[["hb-stan::cv-fit-finalize"]]
folds_dir = "./cv-folds"
run_dir = "./runs/hb-stan-cv-tight"
run_name = "stan_run3"
prior_lkj_eta = 2.0
sigma_scale = 0.5
seed = 7

fields.name: folds_dir
fields.description: Directory of fold subdirs, each containing train.csv.

fields.name: run_dir
fields.description: Output directory; all per-fold and summary outputs written here.

fields.name: run_name
fields.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

fields.name: headers
fields.description: Whether train.csv files have a header row. Default: true.

fields.name: num_samples
fields.description: Post-warmup draws per chain per fold. Default: 1000.

fields.name: num_warmup
fields.description: Warmup iterations per chain per fold. Default: 500.

fields.name: seed
fields.description: Base RNG seed (offset per fold). Omit for non-deterministic.

fields.name: thin
fields.description: Thinning factor. Omit to keep all draws.

fields.name: parallel_chains
fields.description: Run chains in parallel within each fold. Default: true.

fields.name: prior_lkj_eta
fields.description: LKJ prior η, all folds. Default: 1.0.

fields.name: sigma_scale
fields.description: Half-normal σ prior scale, all folds. Default: 1.0.

fields.name: ridge_scale
fields.description: Covariance prior ridge scale, all folds. Default: 1e-6.

fields.name: covariate_input
fields.description: Path to respondent-level covariate CSV. Same file for all folds. Omit for intercept-only.

artifacts.title: Per-fold part-worth utilities
artifacts.description: Individual-level posterior-mean utilities estimated on each fold's training respondents. One file per fold subdirectory.

artifacts.title: Per-fold convergence diagnostics
artifacts.description: Rhat and ESS values for each fold's sampling run. Review to confirm all folds converged before trusting the CV summary.

artifacts.title: Cross-validation summary
artifacts.description: Per-fold log joint posterior (lp__) statistics — the standard metric for comparing HB-Stan model configurations across a CV run. Higher lp__ values indicate better model fit.

fields.name: lp__mean
fields.description: Mean log joint posterior across post-warmup draws for this fold.

fields.name: lp__sd
fields.description: Standard deviation of lp__ across draws.
